value_accrual_summary: STX holders who lock tokens via Proof-of-Transfer (PoX) 'Stacking' receive native BTC rewards sourced from Bitcoin miners who must transfer BTC to Stackers to mine new STX blocks — yield is paid in BTC, not newly minted STX, making it non-inflationary real-yield; STX itself has a fixed emission schedule that will eventually reach a tail emission of 0.5% annually, but Stacking rewards are structurally BTC-denominated miner expenditure, not protocol inflation.

notes.burn_detail: No STX burn mechanism exists in the base protocol. BTC spent by miners is sent to Stackers (not burned), and STX itself is not burned.
notes.staking_apr_source: The ~7.5% figure is a recent approximate market rate for Stacking rewards denominated in BTC-equivalent yield; exact real-time APR varies by cycle and is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from a single authoritative free endpoint. CoinGecko and stacking.club (community tracker) show ranges of 6–10% depending on cycle participation.
notes.inflation_caveat: Stacks does have a block subsidy (STX emission schedule starting at 1,000 STX/block, halving every 4 years aligned with Bitcoin halvings). This inflationary STX goes to miners, NOT stackers. Stackers receive BTC. Therefore staking_emission_inflationary=false for the BTC yield component, but the overall STX supply is still inflationary via miner subsidies, diluting all holders.
notes.pox4_upgrade: The Nakamoto upgrade (SIP-021) and PoX-4 contract (live 2024) changed Stacking mechanics; signers now also participate in consensus, adding a security role to value capture.
notes.staking_apr_pct_caveat: The ~6-7% APR figure (in BTC terms) is a trailing estimate visible on CoinGecko and stacking dashboards; it fluctuates with BTC price, STX price, and total STX locked. Exact current cycle APR requires a live stacking dashboard (e.g., stacking.club) which is free but dynamic — treat 6.5% as a rough mid-2024 reference.
notes.emission_schedule: STX has a declining block-reward schedule (started 1000 STX/block, halving-like reductions) converging to a 0.5%/yr tail emission. New STX issuance goes to miners, NOT to Stackers. Stackers receive BTC from miners.
notes.no_burn: There is no fee-burn mechanism in the current Stacks protocol. Transaction fees are paid in STX and go to miners.
notes.nakamoto_upgrade: The Stacks Nakamoto upgrade (SIP-021, live 2024) changes block production but does not alter the PoX BTC-reward mechanism for Stackers.
